title: Welsh rarebit
subtitle: Sharp cheddar, ale, and mustard on grilled toast
excerpt: A sauce of melted cheddar, Welsh ale, egg yolk, Worcestershire sauce, and English mustard, spread thick on toast and grilled until blistered and brown. Not cheese on toast — the sauce is a separate preparation. Served as a lunch dish, a light supper, or a savoury end to a meal.
sourceNotes: Welsh rarebit is one of the oldest named British dishes, appearing in cookbooks from the early 18th century. It is traditionally made with a Welsh ale and Welsh cheddar or Caerphilly. The egg yolk and Worcestershire sauce version became standard in the 19th century. It is sometimes served as a savoury course at the end of a formal dinner.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The rarebit sauce can be made a day ahead and refrigerated. Spread cold and grill.

content.text: The sauce is thickened to a spreadable paste before going under the grill — it should not be pourable. If it is too liquid it will run off the toast. Chill briefly if needed to firm it before spreading.

Heat the {{beer}} in a small pan over low heat. Add the {{cheddar}} and stir until melted and smooth. Remove from the heat. Stir in the egg yolk from the {{eggs}}, the {{worcestershire-sauce}}, {{english-mustard}}, and {{black-pepper}}. The mixture should be a thick, spreadable paste. Preheat the grill to high. Toast the {{sourdough}} on one side. Flip and spread the rarebit sauce thickly onto the untoasted side. Grill for 3 to 5 minutes, until the surface is blistered and deep brown in spots, and the sauce is bubbling. Serve immediately.

items.cause: Heat too high when melting the cheese
items.fix: Melt on the lowest heat and do not boil. If it splits, remove from the heat and stir vigorously — it usually comes back together

items.symptom: Running off the toast under the grill
items.cause: Sauce too thin, spread before it thickened properly
items.fix: Allow the sauce to cool for a minute before spreading — it thickens slightly as it cools. If still too thin, refrigerate for 5 minutes

items.symptom: Pale and not blistering under the grill
items.cause: Grill not hot enough, or toast too close to the element
items.fix: Preheat the grill for at least 5 minutes on its highest setting. The blistering requires direct intense heat

content.text: Welsh rarebit is a lunch dish, a late-night supper, and a savoury to end a dinner when something rich and sharp is wanted after dessert. It is the most sophisticated use of cheese on toast in the British tradition — made with a sauce rather than slices of cheese, and seasoned correctly it has a depth that straight melted cheese cannot match.
